  • https://spectrum.ieee.org/schaft-robot-company-bought-by-google-darpa-robotics-challenge-winner — SCHAFT: JSK origin, DARPA funding constraint, Google acquisition
  • https://www.caixinglobal.com/2026-03-05/chinas-robot-era-valued-at-over-10-billion-yuan-102419832.html — Robotera >¥10B valuation; Tsinghua IIIS incubation
  • https://pandaily.com/star-dynasty-humanoid-robot-sf-express-20260622 — Robotera >¥4B cumulative raise, SF Express logistics deployment (2026-06)
  • https://www.forbes.com/sites/ywang/2025/08/25/the-700-million-chinese-robot-startup-that-wants-to-take-on-tesla/ — Galaxea AI: Tsinghua/Stanford founding team, Hang Zhao (ex-Waymo) chief science role
  • https://www.caixinglobal.com/2026-04-02/robot-startup-galaxea-ai-raises-291-million-102430297.html — Galaxea ¥2B (~$291M) at >¥20B valuation (2026-04)
  • https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/spirit-ai-lands-280m-to-scale-embodied-ai-through-dirty-data-302697085.html — Spirit AI $280M (2026-02); Yang Gao (Tsinghua AP, Berkeley PhD); 200k+ h data claim
  • https://baike.baidu.com/en/item/Spirit%20AI%20(Hangzhou)%20Technology%20Co.,%20Ltd./33601 — Spirit AI founded 2024-01 (Hangzhou); Han Fengtao + Yang Gao co-founders
  • https://www.psibot.ai/en/about-us/ — PsiBot: Yaodong Yang chief scientist, PKU joint lab, Psi R0/R1
  • https://autonews.gasgoo.com/articles/icv/seeds-psibot-announces-completion-of-2-billion-yuan-financing-2031589417448222721 — PsiBot ¥2B financing, state-backed investors
  • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leju_Robot — Leju: HIT spinout (2016), Leng Xiaokun, Kuavo/HarmonyOS
  • https://www.therobotreport.com/leju-raises-200m-humanoid-production-unitree-unveils-h2-robot/ — Leju $207M pre-IPO round
  • https://www.thewirechina.com/whos_who/zhu-qiuguo-%E6%9C%B1%E7%A7%8B%E5%9B%BD/ — Zhu Qiuguo: ZJU associate professor + DEEP Robotics founder/CEO
  • https://www.zju.edu.cn/english/2025/0313/c75270a3026836/page.htm — ZJU "six little dragons" cultivation narrative, ZJUDancer origin
  • https://github.com/InternRobotics — Shanghai AI Lab OpenRobotLab/InternRobotics open stack
  • https://oceanpang.github.io/ — Jiangmiao Pang: Shanghai AI Lab Embodied AI Center lead
  • https://www.geekwire.com/2025/nvidia-leader-uw-prof-dieter-fox-joins-allen-institute-for-ai-to-lead-new-robotics-initiative/ — Fox: NVIDIA Seattle lab → Ai2 robotics initiative (2025-07)
  • https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/nvidia-open-humanoid-robot-reference-design — GR00T reference humanoid (2026-06-01): Unitree H2 Plus + Sharpa hands; partners Ai2, ETH, Stanford Robotics Center, UCSD ARCLab
  • https://theaiinsider.tech/2025/11/20/sunday-emerges-from-stealth-with-35m-for-household-robot-called-memo/ — Sunday Robotics: Zhao (CEO) & Chi (CTO), $35M Benchmark/Conviction, Memo + Skill Capture Glove
  • https://www.founded.com/sunday-memo-robot-chores-founders/ — Sunday founded 2024, reported ~$1.15B valuation, founders' ALOHA/Diffusion Policy lineage
  • https://h2t.iar.kit.edu/tamim-asfour.php — Asfour: ARMAR family since 1998, KIT H2T
  • https://www.ias.informatik.tu-darmstadt.de/Team/JanPeters — Peters: TU Darmstadt IAS + DFKI SAIROL (since 2022)
  • https://www.tri.global/about-us/dr-russ-tedrake — Tedrake MIT + TRI SVP dual role
  • https://droid-dataset.github.io/ — DROID: 13 institutions, Stanford-led
  • https://robotics-transformer-x.github.io/ — Open X-Embodiment: 21-institution collaboration
  • https://meche.mit.edu/people/faculty/SANGBAE@MIT.EDU — MIT lists Sangbae Kim as "Professor of Mechanical Engineering (On Leave)" (as of 2026-07)
  • https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/meta-hires-mits-sangbae-kim-robotics-architect-brian-heater-mf7ge — Kim signed on at Meta in 2025-03 as "Robotics Architect" in its robotics studio (Heater, 2025-04-16); Kim's own LinkedIn profile/announcement corroborates
  • https://kpertsch.github.io/ — Pertsch self-describes as member of technical staff at Physical Intelligence; DROID/OpenVLA/RoboArena project pages

Landscape: USA & Canada

✓ 已核查更新 2026-07-04landscape-usa

The USA is the center of gravity of Physical AI capital and software: as of 2026-07 it hosts the two most highly valued humanoid makers (Figure at $39B, Tesla's Optimus program), the two most highly valued robot-brain labs (Skild AI at $14B+, Physical Intelligence at ~$5.6B and reportedly raising at $11B), the dominant platform vendor (NVIDIA), and all of the big-tech embodied-AI efforts (Google DeepMind's Gemini Robotics, Meta's Robotics Studio, OpenAI's new robotics division). 2026 milestones include the first US humanoid IPO path (Agility's $2.5B SPAC), Boston Dynamics' production electric Atlas, 1X's consumer NEO shipping from a Hayward factory, and robotics venture funding running ahead of all of 2025 by mid-year. Canada's footprint is smaller — Sanctuary AI in Vancouver plus deployment sites and academic labs. See Landscape: China for the main rival ecosystem and Investment for cross-cutting funding data.

Snapshot: major players (as of 2026-07)

CompanyHQFlagshipFunding / valuation (as of)Status
FigureSan Jose, CAFigure 03 humanoid + Helix VLA>$1B Series C, $39B post-money (2025-09)40 units at BMW Spartanburg (unverified); BotQ factory ramping
Tesla OptimusPalo Alto / Fremont, CAOptimus V3internal (Tesla)several hundred built Q1 2026; V3 line start ~Q3 2026
Boston DynamicsWaltham, MAelectric Atlas (production)Hyundai-owned2026 production run fully committed (Hyundai RMAC + Google DeepMind)
Agility RoboticsSalem, ORDigit v4/v5~$2.5B SPAC (announced 2026-06)9 customer sites, 65k+ operating hours
ApptronikAustin, TXApollo / Apollo 2>$935M Series A total, ~$5B valuation (2026-02)pilots with Mercedes-Benz, GXO, Jabil
1X TechnologiesPalo Alto, CA (Norwegian origin)NEO home humanoidOpenAI/EQT Ventures-backed; reported seeking ~$1B at $10B+ (2025-09, close unverified)full-scale production at Hayward, CA since 2026-04
Physical IntelligenceSan Francisco, CAπ0 → π0.7 foundation models$600M Series B @ $5.6B (2025-11); ~$11B round reported (unverified)licenses models; no hardware
Skild AIPittsburgh, PASkild Brain$1.4B Series C @ >$14B (2026-01)~$30M revenue claimed for 2025 (company figure)
NVIDIASanta Clara, CAIsaac GR00T / Cosmos / Jetson Thorpublicplatform layer for most of the above

Humanoid hardware companies

  • Figure — raised >$1B Series C at a $39B post-money valuation (announced 2025-09-16; lead Parkway Venture Capital, with Brookfield, NVIDIA, Macquarie, Intel Capital, LG Technology Ventures, Salesforce, T-Mobile Ventures, Qualcomm Ventures). Ended its OpenAI partnership in early 2025 and built the in-house Helix VLA. Unveiled Figure 03 (2025-10), redesigned for mass manufacture and home settings; BotQ factory targets ~100,000 robots over four years (company target, as of 2025-10). Deployment proof point: Figure 02 ran daily shifts at BMW Spartanburg for ~11 months, loading 90,000+ parts into production of 30,000+ X3s; ~40 Figure 03 units reported at the same plant (unverified). See Humanoid robots.
  • Tesla Optimus — Tesla disclosed producing "several hundred" Optimus units in Q1 2026 (learning/data-collection phase). Converted the Fremont Model S/X lines (last S/X built 2026-05) to Optimus; Musk guided V3 production start late July–August 2026, initial output "quite slow" (~10,000 unique parts), with mass production targeted late 2026 and a 1M units/year design capacity at Fremont; a second line at Giga Texas is planned with up to 10M/year long-term design capacity (company statements — treat all Tesla timelines as aspirational; prior Optimus dates have slipped). Stated long-run price target $20–30K (unverified).
  • Boston Dynamics (now 100% Hyundai-owned — SoftBank's remaining 9.65% bought out for ~$325M, board approval reported 2026-06-22; verified via KED/Reuters, see BD deep dive) — unveiled the production version of the electric Atlas at CES on 2026-01-05; official spec sheet (2025-12): 1.9 m / 90 kg, 56 DoF, 50 kg instant / 30 kg sustained lift. The entire 2026 Atlas production run is committed to two customers: Hyundai's Robotics Metaplant Application Center (Georgia) and Google DeepMind. Hyundai's $26B US investment plan includes a robot factory sized for ~30,000 robots/year; Hyundai Mobis supplies actuators.
  • Agility Robotics — announced on 2026-06-24 a SPAC merger with Churchill Capital Corp XI at ~$2.5B pre-money valuation, >$620M gross proceeds ($420M Churchill XI trust + ~$200M PIPE led by Foxconn), ticker AGLT on a North American exchange not yet named (Churchill XI itself trades on Nasdaq as CCXI), expected to close in 2026 — set to be the first US humanoid maker to go public. Per its filings: Digit operates at 9 customer facilities with 65,000+ cumulative operating hours; customers include GXO Logistics, Schaeffler, Toyota Motor Manufacturing Canada, and Mercado Libre; >$300M in multi-year Digit v5 orders (milestone-contingent). Digit v5 is pitched as the first "cooperatively safe" humanoid (company claim).
  • Apptronik — closed a $520M Series A extension on 2026-02-11 (total Series A >$935M; total raised ~$1B; ~$5B valuation per CNBC). Backers: B Capital, Google, Mercedes-Benz, PEAK6, plus new investors AT&T Ventures, John Deere, Qatar Investment Authority. Apollo pilots with Mercedes-Benz, GXO, Jabil. Unveiled Apollo 2 on 2026-06-30 alongside "Robot Park," a 90,000 sq ft data-collection and training campus in Austin where Apollo 2 fleets continuously gather real-world manipulation data for models developed with Google DeepMind (as of 2026-07). Spun out of UT Austin's Human Centered Robotics Lab.
  • 1X Technologies (Norwegian-founded, US-headquartered; OpenAI-backed in earlier rounds) — opened NEO pre-orders 2025-10 at $20,000 purchase or $499/month subscription; company claims ~10,000 pre-orders in the first five days (unverified). Full-scale NEO production began 2026-04 at its 58,000 sq ft Hayward, CA factory (200+ staff), first deliveries to US homes promised before end-2026. NEO runs 1X's Redwood generalist model on NVIDIA Jetson Thor; early autonomy is supplemented by human teleoperation, which drew privacy scrutiny.

Robot-brain labs (models, not bodies)

  • Physical Intelligence (π) — founded 2024 (Sergey Levine, Karol Hausman, Chelsea Finn et al.; Berkeley/Stanford/Google-DeepMind roots). Model line: π0 (2024-10), π0-FAST + open π0 weights (2025-02), π0.5 open-world mobile manipulation (2025-04), **π*0.6 trained with the RECAP RL method (2025-11-17; claimed ~2x throughput and ~half the failure rate on hardest tasks; espresso service ran 5:30am-11:30pm ≈ 18h per PI's blog — see PI deep dive), π0.7 "steerable" foundation model (2026-04-16). Funding: $400M Series A @ $2.4B (2024-11), $600M Series B @ ~$5.6B (2025-11, CapitalG + Lux lead); a ~$1B round at $11B valuation** reported in progress as of 2026-H1 (press reports, unverified). Investors include Jeff Bezos, OpenAI, Sequoia, Thrive. See VLA models.
  • Skild AI — Pittsburgh; founded by CMU professors Deepak Pathak and Abhinav Gupta. $1.4B Series C at >$14B valuation (2026-01-14), led by SoftBank with NVentures (NVIDIA), Bezos Expeditions, Macquarie Capital, 1789 Capital, and strategics LG, Schneider Electric, CommonSpirit, Salesforce Ventures (Samsung joined the prior ~$4.5B round in mid-2025 with ~$10M, not the Series C). Sells Skild Brain, pitched as an "omni-bodied" model controlling quadrupeds, humanoids, arms, and mobile manipulators; deployments claimed in security, construction, data centers, warehouses; company claims revenue went 0 → ~$30M during 2025 (company figure, unverified).
  • Adjacent US model labs: World Labs (Fei-Fei Li; spatial/world models — see World models) and Toyota Research Institute's large behavior models work (unverified currency).

NVIDIA's platform play

NVIDIA is positioning as the "Android of generalist robotics" (TechCrunch framing, 2026-01) — supplying compute, simulation, world models, and pretrained robot policies while investing in customers such as Figure (Series B/C) and Skild AI (via NVentures), and supplying onboard compute to 1X, Physical Intelligence partners, and most other US players.

  • GTC 2026-03-16 announcements: Cosmos 3 world foundation model (unifying synthetic world generation, vision reasoning, action simulation); GR00T N1.7 robot foundation model in early access with commercial licensing; GR00T N2 previewed for end-2026; Isaac Lab 3.0; Newton physics engine 1.0 (with Google DeepMind/Disney lineage). Partners named include Figure, Agility, Boston Dynamics, Skild AI, 1X, FANUC, ABB, KUKA, Yaskawa. See Simulation and World models.
  • Jetson Thor (2025) is the de facto onboard compute for US humanoids (used by 1X NEO, Figure-class robots); the Jetson AGX Thor T5000 offers ~2,070 FP4 TFLOPS, 128GB unified memory, 40–130W (as of 2026-05 spec).
  • Isaac GR00T reference humanoid (announced 2026-05-31, GTC Taipei): open reference design pairing a Unitree H2 Plus body (a Chinese platform — notable cross-Pacific dependency) with Sharpa hands, Jetson Thor, and the Isaac GR00T stack; launch users Ai2, ETH Zurich, Stanford Robotics Center, UCSD; available from Unitree late 2026. See Hardware.

Big tech

  • Google DeepMind — the most complete big-tech embodied stack: Gemini Robotics VLA (2025-03), On-Device variant (2025-06), Gemini Robotics 1.5 + ER 1.5 with think-before-act reasoning and cross-embodiment motion transfer (2025-09). Hardware-agnostic partner strategy: deep Apptronik partnership (Apollo), trusted testers Agility, Boston Dynamics, Agile Robots, Enchanted Tools — and it is one of the two buyers of the entire 2026 electric Atlas run, signaling a scaled Gemini-on-Atlas research program. Alphabet also invests via CapitalG (Physical Intelligence lead) and Google (Apptronik).
  • Meta — building a platform play: a "Meta Robotics Studio" team (launched 2025) led by ex-Cruise CEO Marc Whitten, with MIT's Sangbae Kim as Robotics Architect (since 2025-03, on leave from MIT) and ex-Apple robotics-research head Jian Zhang (per Bloomberg-sourced reporting), developing an internal humanoid codenamed "Metabot"; plus the acquisition of Assured Robot Intelligence (closed 2026-05-01), whose co-founders Lerrel Pinto (NYU; ex-Fauna Robotics co-founder) and Xiaolong Wang (UCSD; ex-NVIDIA) joined Meta Superintelligence Labs to work with the Robotics Studio team. Stated strategy: develop sensors, software, and robot AI models licensed to third-party humanoid makers ("Android model") rather than sell robots (as of 2026-05 reporting).
  • OpenAI — re-entered robotics after its 2025 Figure breakup: a dedicated robotics division announced targeting humanoids for infrastructure work and eventually a personal robot; ~11 SF hardware/sim/data/ML roles open as of 2026-06; Aditya Ramesh (DALL-E creator, Sora contributor, previously VP of Research leading Worldsim) is now VP of Robotics (his LinkedIn title + 2026-06 press reports; earlier his personal site described "bootstrapping" the effort), aiming to bring "the intelligence of our video generation models into the physical realm"; hardware/robotics lead Caitlin Kalinowski resigned 2026-03-07 over the Pentagon contract — "surveillance of Americans without judicial oversight and lethal autonomy without human authorization are lines that deserved more deliberation" — a departure OpenAI confirmed while asserting the contract excludes domestic surveillance and autonomous weapons (per Fortune). OpenAI remains an investor in Physical Intelligence and 1X.
  • Amazon — a deployment gravity well rather than a model lab: named among Digit deployment customers, and runs its own warehouse-robotics AI programs (see Organizations).
  • Tesla — vertically integrated: own hardware, own FSD-derived neural nets, own factories as both training ground and first customer (see above).

Academic hubs and open source

  • Stanford — REAL (Shuran Song), IRIS (Chelsea Finn → PI co-founder), Stanford Robotics Center (GR00T reference-robot launch user); co-origin of OpenVLA (7B open VLA trained on 970k demos; outperformed 55B RT-2-X by ~16.5 pts absolute success across 29 tasks at release).
  • UC Berkeley — BAIR / Robot Learning Lab (Pieter Abbeel, Sergey Levine → PI); persistent source of RL-for-robotics talent.
  • CMU (Pittsburgh) — Robotics Institute; direct pipeline into Skild AI; anchors the Pittsburgh robotics cluster.
  • Others — MIT, UCSD (Xiaolong Wang → Meta), NYU (Lerrel Pinto → Meta), UT Austin (→ Apptronik), Ai2 in Seattle (GR00T reference-robot launch user). Hugging Face's LeRobot ecosystem (SmolVLA, 450M params, community datasets) is the main open on-ramp; see Data and Key people.

Canada

  • Sanctuary AI (Vancouver, BC) — the anchor Canadian player. Phoenix humanoid with hydraulic dexterous hands + Carbon control stack. Turbulent 2024–25: CTO Suzanne Gildert left 2024-04, CEO Geordie Rose removed by the board 2024-11, James Wells (ex-CCO) as CEO; pivoted toward industrial deployments and selling teleoperation/dexterity software for third-party platforms. Hiring again as of 2026-01 (~19 open roles, deployment-focused) and claims Phoenix deployed in real manufacturing plants (company claims, largely unverified). Notable patent portfolio in humanoid robotics (IAM analysis).
  • Deployment and supply footprint — Toyota Motor Manufacturing Canada is a named Agility Digit customer site; Ontario's automation corridor (ex-Clearpath/OTTO Motors, acquired by Rockwell 2023) supplies mobile-robot expertise (context, as of 2023).
  • Academics — University of Toronto Robotics Institute / Vector Institute and Mila contribute RL and robot-learning research but no Canadian robot-foundation-model lab at US scale exists as of 2026-07 (assessment, unverified).

Funding climate (as of 2026-07)

  • Robotics startups raised $18.8B globally in 2026 through 2026-06-22 (Crunchbase basis) — already exceeding Crunchbase's ~$15B total for all of 2025 and its 2021 peak (~$14.1B); a broader tally (Briefs) puts 2026 YTD at >$23B vs ~$26B for all of 2025. Figures vary widely by source and definition — treat absolute totals as indicative. See Investment.
  • US megadeals dominate: Figure $1B+ (2025-09), Physical Intelligence $600M (2025-11), Skild $1.4B (2026-01), Apptronik $520M (2026-02), plus the reported PI raise at $11B (unverified).
  • Character of the market: valuations price in a general-purpose future while revenues are tiny ($30M-scale at best); SoftBank, sovereign funds (QIA), Bezos, and corporate strategics (NVIDIA, Google, Samsung, LG, Hyundai, Mercedes, John Deere) are the marginal buyers; public-market access is opening via Agility's SPAC.
  • Twelve commercial humanoid platforms were purchasable or leasable in early 2026 vs ~3 in 2024 (SVRC industry report, unverified). US strength is capital + models + chips; scaled low-cost manufacturing remains the comparative weakness vs China.

Landscape: China

✓ 已核查更新 2026-07-04landscape-china

China is the volume center of the humanoid-robot industry: roughly 12,800 of the world's humanoid units were produced there in 2025 (~90% of global output, per Merics), led by Unitree (5,500 humanoids sold in 2025; STAR Market IPO registration approved July 2026 at a ~$6–6.2B target valuation) and AgiBot/Zhiyuan (5,168 shipped in 2025 per Omdia; 15,000th cumulative unit by June 2026). The ecosystem combines a 60–70% share of the global component supply chain, aggressive price disruption (Unitree's R1 at ~$5,900), state policy that made embodied intelligence a named priority of the 15th Five-Year Plan (2026–30), and high-visibility public milestones — a robot half-marathon whose 2026 winner beat the human world record, and the first World Humanoid Robot Games (Beijing, Aug 2025). Contrast with Landscape: USA; see Humanoid robots for hardware detail and Investment for capital flows.

Why China leads on volume

  • Scale: ~12,800 humanoid units produced in China in 2025 — roughly 90% of global output (Merics, citing industry data; as of 2026-05). NDRC counts ~150 Chinese firms building humanoid robots (other counts run to ~160).
  • Supply chain: Chinese suppliers hold ~63–70% of the global humanoid component supply chain (actuators, reducers, motors, sensors, batteries), with an estimated 30–40% cost advantage. China controls ~69% of rare-earth mining and ~90% of magnet processing — critical for the NdFeB magnets in high-torque actuators. See Hardware.
  • Capital: embodied-intelligence financing in China reached RMB 73.5B (~$10.8B) in 2025; a national venture guidance fund plans ~RMB 1 trillion for AI/robotics/frontier tech over ~20 years.
  • Policy: embodied AI entered the 2025 Government Work Report and the 15th Five-Year Plan (2026–2030) as a top "new industry track"; MIIT stood up a humanoid/embodied-AI standardization committee (Dec 2025), released a first national standards system for the humanoid industry (Mar 2026), and — with SASAC — launched a "real-scene training" special action (2026-06-09) mandating 100+ deployed application scenarios and 10,000-unit-scale deployment capacity by end-2026 (see policy timeline below).
  • Demand-side pull: auto OEMs (BYD, Geely, FAW-VW, Audi FAW, Dongfeng), electronics manufacturers (Foxconn), and logistics firms (SF Express) are piloting humanoids in factories, giving Chinese vendors deployment data US rivals mostly lack. See Data.
  • Caveat: most 2025 unit volume was research/education units and small pilots, not sustained industrial labor. Unitree's own prospectus says >70% of its humanoid sales went to research and educational buyers.

Key players at a glance

Unitree

  • Founded 2016 (Hangzhou) by Wang Xingxing; began in quadrupeds, now the highest-revenue pure-play legged-robot maker. See Key people.
  • Financials (IPO prospectus, as of 2026-03): revenue RMB 392M in 2024 → RMB 1.71B (~$250M) in 2025; 扣非 (excl.-non-recurring) net profit >RMB 600M (~$90M) in 2025, up 674% YoY — rare profitability in this sector (GAAP net profit ~RMB 288M, +204%; the ~RMB 600M is the CSRC-standardized 扣非 measure, higher than GAAP because 2025 non-recurring items were net losses — see Company: Unitree). 480 employees, 175 in R&D.
  • Volumes: 5,500 humanoids sold in 2025; >30,000 quadrupeds shipped 2022–2025-09. Prospectus claims 32.4% of global 2025 humanoid unit shipments (see share-count caveat below). Plans capacity of 75,000 humanoids + 115,000 quadrupeds/yr within five years.
  • Price trajectory: average humanoid selling price fell from ~RMB 593,400 (~$85k) in 2023 to ~RMB 167,600 (~$25k) in 2025, a ~72% drop, while gross margin rose to ~60%. R1 launched 2025-07-25 at RMB 39,900 (~$5,900): 1.21 m, 25 kg, 26 DoF, hot-swappable battery, onboard multimodal model (UnifoLM).
  • Capital markets: June 2025 Series C at RMB 12.7B (~$1.7B) post-money (Alibaba, Tencent, China Mobile, Ant Group, Geely). Filed for Shanghai STAR Market IPO 2026-03-20 seeking RMB 4.2B (~$610–620M); listing committee approved 2026-06-01 in a record 73 days — first "embodied AI" company cleared for A-shares; CSRC registration approved 2026-07-02 (reported 2026-07-03; $618M raise); target post-IPO valuation ~RMB 42B (~$6–6.2B). Underwriting, pricing, and share subscription are the remaining steps; press reports suggest a STAR Market debut as early as late July 2026 (unverified).
  • Dominated the first World Humanoid Robot Games (2025-08): 11 medals incl. 4 golds (400 m, 1,500 m, 100 m hurdles, 4×100 m relay); its H1 hit 4.78 m/s in competition. See Locomotion.

AgiBot / Zhiyuan Robotics

  • Founded 2023-02 (Shanghai) by Peng Zhihui ("Zhihui Jun," ex-Huawei "genius youth") and others; fastest scale-up in the sector.
  • Volumes: shipped 5,168 humanoids in 2025 — 39% of the global market per Omdia, which puts total 2025 global shipments at ~13,000 (conflicts with Unitree's 32.4% self-claim; the two counts likely use different category boundaries — both "(competing-methodology)" claims). Cumulative 10,000th unit rolled out 2026-03-30; 15,000th (a G2 industrial unit) followed 2026-06-28 — the 5,000→10,000 step took ~3 months.
  • Models/data: released GO-1 generalist embodied foundation model 2025-03 (ViLLA architecture: InternVL2.5-2B VLM + latent planner + diffusion action expert), trained on >1M robot trajectories (2,976 h, 217 tasks, 100 dual-arm robots — the AgiBot World dataset, one of the largest open real-robot manipulation corpora); GO-1 weights later open-sourced on Hugging Face (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0); GO-2 followed in April 2026 (unverified). See VLA models and Data.
  • Capital: strategic round 2025-08 with LG Electronics and Mirae Asset (amount undisclosed; LG's first embodied-AI bet), plus BYD and Hillhouse earlier; valuation reported >RMB 15B mid-2025. Acquired ~63.6% control of A-share-listed Swancor Advanced Materials for ~RMB 2.1B (~$290M) in July 2025 — denied it was a backdoor listing; Swancor stock hit an 11-day limit-up streak. Reported HK IPO target Q3 2026 at HK$40–50B ($5.1–6.4B) (unverified).

UBTech

  • Shenzhen; listed on HKEX (9880) Dec 2023 — the earliest publicly traded Chinese humanoid pure-play.
  • 2025 results (reported 2026-03-31): revenue RMB 2.001B (+53.3% YoY); full-size humanoid segment RMB 820M, up 2,203.7% YoY, on 1,079 units delivered — first company to exceed 1,000 full-size humanoid deliveries in a year. Gross margin 37.7%; net loss narrowed ~32% to RMB 790M (still loss-making).
  • Walker S2 (industrial humanoid, autonomous hot-swap battery): mass production and first several-hundred-unit batch delivery began 2025-11; cumulative Walker-series orders >RMB 800M (~$112M) since early 2025, incl. a RMB 250M order (2025-09) and RMB 159M for a data-collection center in Zigong. Capacity plan: 5,000 units/yr by 2026, 10,000 by 2027.
  • Customers/pilots: BYD, Foxconn, Geely, FAW-Volkswagen Qingdao, Audi FAW, BAIC New Energy, Dongfeng Liuzhou, SF Express — mostly automotive final-assembly, logistics, and data-collection roles.
  • UWORLD U1 consumer line (unveiled 2026-06-30 at UBTech's Shenzhen global launch event): "ultra-bionic" companion humanoids with lifelike silicone skin, marketed for companionship, eldercare, reception, and premium domestic service. Three models — U1 Lite (semi-torso), U1 Pro, U1 Ultra — priced from RMB 119,800 (~$16,700); UBTech claimed 13,361 cumulative orders at launch (company-reported; TechNode cites ~11,000+ secured), first deliveries from mid-September 2026 (as of 2026-07). A consumer-market pivot alongside the industrial Walker line.

Galbot

  • Beijing; founded 2023-05, spun out of Peking University robotics research (founder Wang He). Product is the G1 semi-humanoid — wheeled base + dual arms, optimized for retail picking rather than legs.
  • Deployment: runs fully autonomous "Galbot Store" convenience stores — claimed world's first humanoid-operated store in Beijing — expanded to 30+ cities, plus 24/7 robot-staffed pharmacies in Beijing (10+). One of the few Chinese players with recurring commercial operations rather than hardware sales.
  • Capital (as of 2025-12): raised >$300M in a round valuing it at $3B (record for China's humanoid sector at the time); total funding ~$800M. Earlier 2025: RMB 1.1B (~$153M) led by CATL and Puquan Capital; a ~$151M round involving Bosch's investment arm (2025-07). Picked CITIC Securities, Huatai, and UBS for a planned 2026 Hong Kong IPO (Bloomberg, 2025-12).
  • Won a gold medal at the 2025 World Humanoid Robot Games (retail/service scenario events).

Fourier (Fourier Intelligence)

  • Shanghai; founded 2015 in rehabilitation robotics; pivoted to humanoids with GR-1 (2023) and GR-2 (2024).
  • GR-3 (unveiled 2025-08): first "care-bot" positioning — 165 cm, 71 kg, 55 DoF, soft-touch exterior, 31 pressure sensors, full-sensory (audio/visual/tactile) interaction, emotion recognition; ~$27,500 B2B price in China (as of 2025-08); ~3 h battery.
  • Strategy is differentiated: healthcare, rehab, and eldercare rather than factory labor — a bet on China's aging demographics.

EngineAI

  • Shenzhen; founded late 2023 by ex-XPeng robotics engineers (CEO Zhao Tongyang).
  • Milestone: world's first humanoid robot front flip (PM01, 2025-02) — part of a wave of Chinese acrobatic-locomotion demos.
  • Price disruption: PM01 commercial version at RMB 88,000 (~$12k); 1.38 m, ~40 kg, 23–24 DoF.
  • Capital: ~RMB 1B (~$139M) raised across pre-A++ (Rocket Capital, XPeng) and A1 (JD.com, CATL Capital, Yintai, Tsinghua Holdings) rounds announced 2025-07; A1+/A2 rounds completed by 2025-12 alongside launch of full-size T800 (factory claims one T800 every 15 minutes (unverified)). Filed confidentially for a Hong Kong IPO 2026-06 (Bloomberg).

X Square Robot and AI² Robotics (Shenzhen model-first pair)

  • Two Shenzhen embodied-AI startups announced ~RMB 20B (~$2.8B) valuations on the same day (2026-06-29) — a marker of how fast capital is compounding behind Chinese "model-first" robotics companies (both founded 2023).
  • X Square Robot: closed four consecutive rounds ending in a Series C (IDG participated in the C; HongShan and Xiaomi backed multiple rounds) at a >$2.8B (RMB 20B) valuation; claims to be the only Chinese embodied-AI company with lead-round backing at different stages from all four of Alibaba, ByteDance, Meituan, and Xiaomi (company release, as of 2026-06). Builds the wheeled Quanta humanoid series (flagship X2) and the WALL model family (WALL-OSS open weights, WALL-B unified foundation model — see VLA models); deployments include AI cleaning services with 58.com in Shenzhen and Beijing and month-long in-home trials ("X Family Member Program").
  • AI² Robotics (智平方): raised ~RMB 5B (~$735M) in new financing at a RMB 20B+ valuation (2026-06-29), on top of a Series B earlier in 2026 at a ~RMB 10B (~$1.4B) valuation. Builds the AlphaBot wheeled-humanoid series (34+ DoF) on its "Alpha Brain" foundation model for industrial, biotech, public-service and retail settings; targets scaling output from ~1,000 units (2025) to ~10,000 in 2026 (company claim).
  • Chinese trade press reported the two publicly disputing which was the first Greater Bay Area embodied-AI company to hit the RMB 20B valuation mark — both claimed the title the same day (Gasgoo) — itself a sign of valuation-led positioning in the sector.

Xiaomi and XPeng (consumer-electronics / EV entrants)

  • Xiaomi: unveiled CyberOne in 2022; showed CyberOne V2 at a 2026 investor day doing social/interaction tasks after claiming a 90.2% success rate in autonomous automotive-assembly pilot tasks (company figure, unverified). Open-sourced the "Xiaomi-Robotics-0" embodied model in 2026 (unverified). No public specs, price, or mass-production timeline as of 2026-07.
  • XPeng: unveiled next-gen IRON at AI Day 2025-11-05 — 82 DoF (22 per hand), humanoid spine + bionic muscle + flexible skin, 3× in-house Turing chips (~3,000 TOPS), first robot with an all-solid-state battery; runs a "VLT+VLA+VLM" stack sharing XPeng's automotive VLA 2.0 lineage. The catwalk demo was so smooth it triggered viral "person in a suit" accusations, which XPeng rebutted by cutting the leg covering on camera. Factory groundbreaking Q1 2026; mass production targeted end-2026, initial roles in showrooms, museums, malls; CEO He Xiaopeng has floated ~1M units/yr ambition by 2030 (aspirational). XPeng is also an EngineAI investor.
  • Automakers-as-robot-makers is a distinct Chinese pattern (XPeng, Xiaomi, GAC, Chery, BYD as investor/customer): they bring EV supply chains, mass-manufacturing muscle, and in-house AI chips. See Organizations.

Government policy and subsidies

  • Timeline:
    • 2023-11: MIIT "Guiding Opinions on Innovative Development of Humanoid Robots" — mass production by 2025, world-leading industry by 2027.
    • 2025-03: embodied AI named in the national Government Work Report for the first time; "AI+" strategy frames robots as intelligent terminals for the whole economy.
    • 2025-12: MIIT establishes Humanoid Robot & Embodied Intelligence Standardization Technical Committee.
    • 2026-03: 15th Five-Year Plan (2026–2030) designates robotics a strategic emerging industry and embodied intelligence a top-ten "new industry track"; first national humanoid standards system released.
    • 2026-06-09: MIIT + SASAC jointly launch the 2026 Humanoid Robot & Embodied Intelligence Real-Scene Training Special Action (实景实训专项行动) — the explicit pivot from "technology verification" to "operation mode": by end-2026, routine (normalized) deployment in representative real scenarios, 100+ high-value application scenarios distilled, and "10,000-unit-scale" deployment capacity. Six tasks (real-scene training spaces, industry–academia application consortiums, practical work-skill development, deployment verification, factor guarantees, experience codification) across manufacturing, inspection, maintenance, warehousing/logistics, dining/retail, healthcare/eldercare, work safety, emergency response and disaster relief; anchored in 10 provinces/municipalities (Beijing, Tianjin, Shanghai, Jiangsu, Zhejiang, Shandong, Hubei, Hunan, Guangdong, Sichuan) plus central SOEs — each province to stand up ≥20 training scenarios spanning ≥2 domains, each participating central SOE ≥10. Per the notice itself (工信厅联科函〔2026〕256号, dated 2026-06-03, published 2026-06-09): work plans were due to MIIT/SASAC by 2026-06-30, completion summaries with performance data by 2026-11-30.
  • Money (as of 2026-04): ~RMB 1 trillion (~$137B) national VC guidance fund for AI/robotics over two decades; Shenzhen RMB 10B AI & robotics fund (2025); Beijing Robot Industry Fund (a Galbot investor); local subsidies up to 30% of automation project costs plus purchase discounts for humanoids; estimates of ~$20B in direct subsidies to the sector (single-source, unverified).
  • State demand: government-linked buyers (data-collection centers, state utilities, municipal pilots) are a meaningful share of early orders — e.g., UBTech's RMB 159M Zigong data-collection-center order.
  • State Grid 2026 embodied-robot procurement (reported 2026-04): State Grid Corp.'s internal "2026 Embodied Intelligence Development Plan" — leaked to Jiemian (2026-04-24), not a published tender — budgets RMB 6.8B (~$995M) for 2026 alone: ~8,500 robots comprising 5,000 quadrupeds (RMB 1.5B; substation/terrain inspection), 3,000 dual-arm robots (RMB 1.8B), and 500 humanoids (RMB 2.5B — ~RMB 5M/unit, implying specialized insulated live-line/UHV platforms, not commodity units), plus ~RMB 1B for R&D/talent. Reported phasing: Q1 pilots → Q3 scale procurement → Q4 supplements; sector total >RMB 10B in 2026 once China Southern Power Grid and local utilities follow. Deep Robotics, Unitree, AgiBot, UBTech and Fourier are named as expected suppliers (industry-media reporting, unverified). Morgan Stanley's 2026-06 forecast note cited this as the landmark order behind raising its 2026 China shipment forecast to 50K units (see Humanoids). Caveat: all figures trace to that single internal planning document; no public tender awards confirming the humanoid line item had surfaced as of 2026-07.
  • Analysts (Merics, East Asia Forum) frame the policy as deliberately replaying the EV playbook: subsidize supply, seed demand, let brutal domestic price competition select winners, then export.

Supply-chain advantage

  • Actuators + motion systems are the dominant humanoid cost center (estimates range ~40–70% of BOM depending on design); China makes most of the world's harmonic/planetary reducers, frameless motors, and NdFeB magnets at scale.
  • One analysis estimates building Tesla's Optimus Gen 2 without Chinese suppliers would raise BOM from ~$46k to ~$131k (~3×) (single-source, unverified). See Landscape: USA for the mirror-image dependency problem.
  • Yangtze River Delta (Hangzhou/Shanghai/Suzhou) hosts a vertically integrated cluster; Unitree builds motors, reducers, and sensors in-house with suppliers within a ~2-hour logistics radius. The Pearl River Delta (Shenzhen) cluster hosts UBTech, EngineAI, Honor and the electronics supply base.
  • Rare-earth export controls (2025) demonstrated leverage: humanoid-grade actuators depend on Chinese magnet processing (~90% global share).
  • Goldman Sachs notes Chinese component suppliers are building humanoid capacity ahead of confirmed orders — capacity overshoot risk, but also a structural moat if demand arrives.

Public milestones

EventDateResult
Beijing E-Town robot half-marathon (1st)2025-04-19Tiangong Ultra (X-Humanoid) wins in 2:40:42; only 6 of 21 robots finish; ~12,000 human runners alongside
World Humanoid Robot Games (1st)2025-08-14→17, Beijing280 teams, 16 countries; Unitree 4 golds/11 medals; X-Humanoid tops golds; Galbot gold in scenario events
Beijing E-Town robot half-marathon (2nd)2026-04-19Honor's "Lightning" wins in 50:26, beating the official human world record (Kiplimo's 57:20, Lisbon 2026-03; his faster 56:42 Barcelona run was never ratified) by ~7 min; 300+ robots on a parallel course; top 3 all Honor; a 48:19 Honor run penalized 20% for tele-operation
  • Read as marketing + stress-testing: the marathons forced year-over-year progress in thermal management, endurance, and autonomy (2026 winner ran autonomously; remote-controlled entrants' times multiplied by 1.2). One-year improvement from 2:40 to 0:50 is the clearest public benchmark of Chinese locomotion iteration speed. See Locomotion and State of the art.
  • The 50:26 result is confirmed well beyond Chinese state media — Scientific American, CBS, NPR, NBC, and PBS all covered it — but with caveats: "Lightning" (rendered "Flash" in some translations; liquid-cooled motors adapted from Honor's smartphone tech) clipped a barricade and fell late in the race and was set upright by its handlers; the robot course was dedicated, pre-mapped and rehearsed, with support crews trailing every entrant; and only ~38% of the 300+ robot entries ran autonomously. Roboticists quoted by Scientific American cautioned the feat demonstrates purpose-built running hardware, not deployable autonomy.
  • Other spectacle milestones: CCTV Spring Festival Gala dancing Unitree H1s (2025-01), EngineAI front flip (2025-02), robot kickboxing leagues (2025).

Price-disruption dynamics

RobotVendorLaunchPrice (as of launch)
H1 (full-size)Unitree2023~$90k
G1 (1.32 m)Unitree2024-05RMB 99k (~$16k)
PM01 (1.38 m)EngineAI2024-12RMB 88k (~$12k)
R1 (1.21 m)Unitree2025-07RMB 39,900 (~$5,900); R1 Air repriced to RMB 29,900 (~$4,200; ~$4,900 international) 2026-06
GR-3 (care)Fourier2025-08~$27.5k B2B
  • Unitree's fleet-average humanoid price fell ~70% in two years ($85k → $25k, 2023→2025) while the company became profitable — evidence the cost curve, not subsidy alone, is driving prices down.
  • Dynamics mirror the Chinese EV/solar/drone playbook: dozens of funded entrants, component commoditization, sub-$10k entry hardware expanding the developer/education market, thin margins forcing consolidation. Expect a shakeout among the ~150 humanoid firms; IPO proceeds (Unitree, UBTech, Galbot, EngineAI, AgiBot) are the war chests.
  • Open question: cheap hardware ≠ useful labor. Most sub-$20k units are R&D/education/performance platforms; industrial units (Walker S2) still sell for six figures and rely on structured tasks. The binding constraint is autonomy software and data, not price. See Open problems.

Cross-cutting caveats

  • Conflicting market-share numbers: Unitree claims 32.4% of 2025 global humanoid shipments (prospectus); Omdia credits AgiBot with 39% on 5,168 units against a ~13,000-unit global total — a denominator under which Unitree's 5,500 units would be ~42%, not 32.4%. With Merics separately putting Chinese production at ~12,800 units (~90% of global), the counts cannot share a methodology — treat all 2025 share figures as methodology-dependent.
  • Company-reported delivery, success-rate, and capacity numbers (UBTech targets, Xiaomi 90.2%, EngineAI 15-min takt) are largely unaudited marketing claims until IPO prospectuses force disclosure.
  • Chinese "delivery" figures often include sales to research institutes, rental/performance fleets, and state-linked data-collection centers — not equivalent to sustained industrial deployment hours.

  • https://www.news.cn/tech/20260612/a069b3a9948b4e0aaaa94cf3d618e545/c.html — news.cn (Xinhua site, carrying 人民邮电报) on the special action: six tasks, focus scenarios, 100+ high-value scenarios and 10,000-unit-scale capacity by end-2026, ten-province + central-SOE footprint.
  • https://restofworld.org/2026/unitree-china-humanoid-robot-shanghai-ipo/ — Unitree IPO filing (2026-03-20), $610M raise, revenue/profit, 5,500 humanoids in 2025, price decline $85k→$25k, 32.4% share claim, capacity plans.

  • https://www.scmp.com/business/banking-finance/article/3347365/chinas-unitree-robotics-rides-humanoid-tide-it-targets-us610m-ipo — independent confirmation of prospectus figures: RMB 1.71B 2025 revenue (+335%), adjusted net profit RMB 600M.
  • https://www.stcn.com/article/detail/3735182.html — Securities Times: Unitree GAAP net-profit series 2023 −¥11M / 2024 ~¥95M / 2025 ~¥288M ("近3亿") — distinguishes GAAP from the ~¥600M 扣非 figure.
  • https://www.stcn.com/article/detail/3688950.html — Securities Times filing-stage: 2025 扣非净利润 "超6亿, +674.29%" — the ~¥600M measure is 扣非 (excl. non-recurring items).
  • https://www.techtimes.com/articles/317632/20260602/unitree-ipo-cleared-agibot-hits-10000-units-china-humanoid-robot-duopoly-takes-shape.htm — IPO approval 2026-06-01; AgiBot 10,000th unit.
  • https://kraneshares.com/a-complete-guide-to-unitree-robotics-2026-ipo-why-it-matters-for-star-market-etf-kstr-humanoid-robotics-etf-koid/ — Unitree Series C (RMB 12.7B post-money, June 2025) and investor list.
  • https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-07-25/china-s-unitree-r1-is-a-humanoid-robot-costing-less-than-6-000 — Unitree R1 launch at RMB 39,900 (~$5,900), 2025-07-25 (corrects the RMB 39,999 figure in some aggregators).
  • https://www.scmp.com/tech/article/3349489/chinas-unitree-debut-cheapest-humanoid-robot-globally-alibaba-site-sources — R1 Air repriced to RMB 29,900; AliExpress international debut (2026-06).
  • https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/ubtech-humanoid-robot-walker-s2-begins-mass-production-and-delivery-with-orders-exceeding-800-million-yuan-302616924.html — Walker S2 mass production (Nov 2025), RMB 800M orders, customers, 5,000/10,000 capacity plan.
  • https://autonews.gasgoo.com/articles/icv/ubtech-2025-report-card-revenue-from-full-size-humanoid-robots-grows-over-22-fold-2039900685372407808 — UBTech 2025 results: RMB 2.0B revenue, RMB 820M humanoid segment (+2,203.7%), 1,079 units, loss narrowed to RMB 790M.
  • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AgiBot — AgiBot founding, GO-1 (Mar 2025) and shipment timeline.
  • https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/omdia-ranks-agibot-no1-worldwide-in-humanoid-robot-shipments-in-2025-302656788.html — Omdia: AgiBot 5,168 units shipped in 2025, 39% share, ~13,000-unit global total.
  • https://www.therobotreport.com/agibot-rolls-out-10000th-humanoid-robot/ — AgiBot 10,000th cumulative unit (2026-03-30) and production-ramp timeline.
  • https://www.agibot.com/article/231/detail/82.html — AgiBot 15,000th robot (a G2 industrial unit) off the line, 2026-06-28.
  • https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.06669 — AgiBot World Colosseo paper: >1M trajectories, 217 tasks; GO-1 ViLLA model.
  • https://roboticsandautomationnews.com/2025/08/04/agibot-secures-strategic-investment-from-lg-electronics-and-mirae-asset/93485/ — LG/Mirae Asset strategic investment in AgiBot (Aug 2025).
  • https://technode.com/2025/07/10/agibot-denies-backdoor-listing-via-swancor-advanced-materials-acquisition/ — Swancor ~RMB 2.1B control acquisition; backdoor-listing denial.
  • https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/galbot-secures-over-300-million-in-new-funding-breaking-records-with-3-billion-valuation-in-chinas-humanoid-robot-sector-302647204.html — Galbot $300M round, $3B valuation, $800M total (Dec 2025).
  • https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-12-12/humanoid-robot-maker-galbot-said-to-pick-banks-for-2026-hk-ipo — Galbot 2026 HK IPO preparation.
  • https://www.therobotreport.com/galbot-picks-up-153m-commercialize-g1-semi-humanoid/ — Galbot RMB 1.1B CATL-led round; G1 store/pharmacy deployments.
  • https://technode.com/2025/08/07/fourier-intelligence-unveils-the-care-bot-gr-3-its-first-full-sized-companion-humanoid-robot/ — Fourier GR-3 specs, care positioning, ~$27.5k price.
  • https://www.prnewswire.com/apac/news-releases/x-square-robot-secures-four-consecutive-financing-rounds-surpasses-us2-8-billion-valuation-in-push-for-physical-ai-foundation-models-302813098.html — X Square Robot company release (2026-06-29): four rounds through Series C, >$2.8B/RMB 20B valuation, IDG/HongShan/Xiaomi, WALL models, 58.com + X Family Member deployments.
  • https://siliconangle.com/2026/06/29/chinese-robotics-outfits-ai2-robotics-x-square-robots-secure-funding-2-8b-valuation/ — both companies at ~$2.8B (2026-06-29); AI² ~$735M round; AlphaBot/"Alpha Brain"; Quanta X2.
  • https://autonews.gasgoo.com/articles/icv/seeds-ai-robotics-secures-nearly-5-billion-yuan-in-new-financing-2071594874301100032 — AI² Robotics ~RMB 5B new financing at RMB 20B+ (2026-06-29); investor mix; Feb 2026 Series B.
  • https://www.therobotreport.com/ai2-robotics-raises-series-b-funding-advance-alphabot-embodied-ai/ — AI² Robotics Series B (~RMB 1.2B at RMB 10B/$1.4B valuation, 2026-02); AlphaBot wheeled humanoid; production scaling from 1,000 units/yr (2025) to 10,000 in 2026.
  • https://www.therobotreport.com/engineai-releases-pm01-humanoid-robot-for-commercial-educational-use/ — EngineAI PM01 specs and RMB 88k price.
  • https://theaiinsider.tech/2025/07/21/chinas-engineai-raises-139-million-in-fresh-funding-for-humanoid-robots/ — EngineAI ~RMB 1B ($139M) rounds; XPeng/JD/CATL investors.
  • https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-12/humanoid-robot-manufacturer-engineai-is-said-to-file-for-hong-kong-ipo — EngineAI confidential HK IPO filing (June 2026).
  • https://www.xpeng.com/news/019a56f54fe99a2a0a8d8a0282e402b7 — XPeng official: IRON specs (82 DoF, 3 Turing chips/3,000 TOPS, solid-state battery), VLA 2.0, late-2026 mass production.
  • https://www.humanoidsdaily.com/news/xiaomi-debuts-cyberone-v2-at-investor-day-moving-beyond-the-factory-floor — Xiaomi CyberOne V2 demo, 90.2% assembly pilot claim, no production timeline.
  • https://merics.org/en/report/embodied-ai-chinas-ambitious-path-transform-its-robotics-industry — policy timeline, RMB 1T guidance fund, ~150 firms, 12,800 units ≈90% of 2025 global production, 30% local subsidies.
  • https://thediplomat.com/2026/03/chinas-new-five-year-plan-prioritizes-robotics-the-world-should-pay-attention/ — 15th Five-Year Plan robotics/embodied-intelligence designation; MIIT standards committee (Dec 2025) and national standards (Mar 2026).
  • https://eastasiaforum.org/2026/04/23/beijing-bets-on-embodied-intelligence-to-secure-structural-power/ — RMB 73.5B 2025 embodied-AI financing; strategy framing.
  • https://technode.com/2025/04/21/beijing-hosts-worlds-first-half-marathon-for-humanoid-robots-tiangong-ultra-wins-in-two-hours-40-minutes/ — 2025 robot half-marathon: Tiangong Ultra 2:40, 6/21 finishers.
  • https://www.irunfar.com/2026-beijing-e-town-half-marathon-humanoid-robot-beats-human-world-record — 2026 race: Honor "Lightning" 50:26 vs 57:20 human WR; 300+ robots; tele-op penalty.
  • https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/a-humanoid-robot-beat-the-human-half-marathon-record-at-a-beijing-race-but-what-did-it-actually-prove/ — independent confirmation of the 50:26 result and Honor as maker, plus caveats: barricade crash/handler assist, pre-mapped rehearsed course, ~38% autonomy rate, expert skepticism.
  • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beijing_E-Town_Half-Marathon — 2026 edition roundup: 300+ robots, 12,000+ human runners, top-3 all Honor Lightning (50:26 / 50:56 / 53:01), 1.2x remote-control penalty, 48:19 penalized run, Kiplimo 57:20 comparison.
  • https://worldathletics.org/competitions/world-athletics-label-road-races/news/jacob-kiplimo-half-marathon-world-record-lisbon — official half-marathon WR 57:20 (Kiplimo, Lisbon 2026-03-08); his 56:42 Barcelona 2025 run was not ratified.
  • https://www.cnbc.com/2025/08/18/world-humanoid-robot-games-china-tesla-unitree.html — World Humanoid Robot Games (2025-08-14→17), 280 teams/16 countries, Unitree performance.
  • https://www.koreatimes.co.kr/world/20250819/chinas-unitree-x-humanoid-top-medal-total-in-the-worlds-first-humanoid-robot-games — Unitree/X-Humanoid medal table.
  • https://www.mckinsey.com/industries/industrials/our-insights/turning-humanoid-supply-chain-constraints-into-billion-dollar-wins — humanoid supply-chain concentration and actuator cost share.
  • https://www.humanoidsdaily.com/news/goldman-sachs-chinese-suppliers-aggressively-building-humanoid-robot-capacity-ahead-of-orders — Chinese supplier capacity build-out ahead of orders.
  • https://blog.robozaps.com/b/economics-of-humanoid-robot-production — Optimus-without-China BOM estimate ($46k→$131k) (unverified aggregator).
  • https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/ubtech-launches-uworld-u1-the-worlds-first-full-size-mass-produced-ultra-bionic-humanoid-robot-302815272.html — UBTech UWORLD U1 launch (2026-06-30): three models, RMB 119,800 base price, 13,361 orders claimed, mid-Sept deliveries (company press release).
  • https://technode.com/2026/07/01/ubtech-unveils-consumer-humanoid-robot-u1-says-orders-secure-11000-ahead-of-first-deliveries/ — independent report on U1 consumer launch and order tally (~11,000+).
  • https://news.futunn.com/en/post/75500836/robotics-sector-surges-unitree-technology-s-star-market-ipo-registration — Unitree IPO registration effective; pricing/subscription next, possible late-July 2026 debut (press report).
  • https://m.jiemian.com/article/14311565.html — Jiemian (2026-04-24, the original exclusive): "国家电网计划采购具身智能设备约8500台，总投资约68亿元"; primary source all other coverage traces to.
  • https://www.scmp.com/economy/china-economy/article/3351323/china-plans-invest-billions-robot-army-run-its-power-grid — SCMP (2026-04-24) on Jiemian's report of State Grid's internal 2026 plan: RMB 6.8B, ~8,500 robots, sector >RMB 10B in 2026 incl. China Southern Power Grid.
  • https://eu.36kr.com/en/p/3780742359243776 — 36Kr (2026-04-24): breakdown 5,000 quadrupeds/RMB 1.5B, 500 humanoids/RMB 2.5B, 3,000 dual-arm/RMB 1.8B, ~RMB 1B R&D (0.8 R&D + 0.2 talent); Q1/Q3/Q4 phasing; suppliers Deep Robotics, Unitree, AgiBot, UBTech, Fourier (its English text mis-renders 智元/AgiBot as "Zhipu"; Chinese coverage, e.g. 21jingji 2026-04-29, confirms 智元).
  • https://www.humanoidsdaily.com/news/state-grid-s-995-million-bet-8-500-robots-to-spearhead-deployment-year-one-in-energy — secondary recap (2026-04-29); the same document's 2027 80%-penetration and 2030 full-autonomy targets, State Grid's own ROI claims (2–3 yr payback), no independent verification.
  • https://finance.biggo.com/news/cba1aa5c-4279-40d3-88dc-a64032a31283 — Morgan Stanley note (2026-06-23) citing the ~$1B State Grid order as an early-commercialization catalyst alongside its 28K→50K 2026 China forecast raise (secondary).

Landscape: Europe, Japan, Korea & Rest of World

✓ 已核查更新 2026-07-04landscape-row

Outside the US and China, Physical AI is led by a handful of well-capitalized European startups (1X, NEURA Robotics, Wandercraft), Japan's industrial-robotics incumbents pivoting toward AI plus a state-backed foundation-model consortium, and Korea's chaebol conglomerates (Samsung, LG, Hyundai, Doosan) buying and building their way into humanoids. As of 2026-06 the region's headline events are NEURA's up-to-$1.4B Series C (Europe's largest robotics round), Hyundai taking 100% ownership of Boston Dynamics, Japan's SoftBank–NEC–Sony–Honda "Japan AI Foundation Model Development" JV (operating company: Noetra) with up to ¥1T in state backing, Korea's ">$1 trillion" triple-axis AI investment drive (announced 2026-06-29: chips + AI data centers + physical AI), and Mobileye's $900M acquisition of Israel's Mentee Robotics.

Europe

CompanyHQFocusStatus (as of 2026-06)
1X TechnologiesMoss, Norway (+ Bay Area ops/factory)NEO home humanoidFull-scale production started 2026-04; $20K purchase or $499/mo
NEURA RoboticsMetzingen, Germany4NE1 humanoid, MAiRA cobot, MiPA mobile manipulatorSeries C up to $1.4B (2026-06); >$1B orderbook
WandercraftParis, FranceCalvin industrial humanoid, exoskeletonsRenault partner; ~10 Calvins deployed by end-2026 (plan)
PAL RoboticsBarcelona, SpainTALOS, Kangaroo biped, TIAGo ProKangaroo on sale; new arm platform at ICRA 2026
HumanoidLondon, UKHMND 01 mobile manipulatorAlpha unveiled 2025-09; Beta planned Q3 2026

1X Technologies (Norway/US)

  • Founded 2014 as Halodi Robotics (Moss, Norway); OpenAI Startup Fund, EQT Ventures and Tiger Global among backers. NEO Beta unveiled 2024-08; NEO Gamma 2025-02.
  • Opened NEO pre-orders 2025-10: $20,000 purchase or $499/month subscription ($200 deposit); company reported ~10,000 pre-orders in the first five days (company figure, unverified).
  • Struck a deal (2025-12) to also deploy its "home" humanoids into factories and warehouses — hedging the consumer bet with industrial revenue.
  • Began full-scale NEO production 2026-04 at a 58,000 sq ft factory in Hayward, California (200+ staff, as of 2026-04).
  • NEO hardware: ~30 kg soft-bodied frame (vs ~57 kg Tesla Optimus), 22-DoF hands, tendon-driven actuation; autonomy backstopped by human teleoperation for chores it can't yet do — see Humanoid robots and Data on the teleop-as-data-flywheel strategy.
  • Reported (2025-09) to be raising up to $1B at a ≥$10B valuation, up from ~$820M after its 2024 $100M Series B (press reports, unverified; no closed round confirmed as of 2026-07).

NEURA Robotics (Germany)

  • Founded 2019 (David Reger); "cognitive robotics" full-stack: 4NE1 humanoid, MAiRA cognitive cobot arm, MiPA household/mobile manipulator, plus the Neuraverse shared robot-learning ecosystem and "NEURA Gyms" data-collection facilities.
  • Series C of up to $1.4B announced 2026-06-10 — the largest round ever for a European robotics company. Lead: Tether; participants include Qualcomm, Amazon, NVIDIA, Bosch, Schaeffler, imec.xpand, Lingotto, InterAlpen and the European Investment Bank. Reported valuation ~$7B (Bloomberg/CNBC reports; not company-confirmed). Round is milestone-contingent — the full "up to $1.4B" depends on undisclosed performance milestones; it is a ceiling, not cash banked. Prior round: €120M Series B, 2025-01, led by Lingotto.
  • Orderbook and deployment pipeline stated at >$1B (as of 2026-06); large-scale 4NE1 shipments targeted from late 2026; stated goal of scaling manufacturing across Germany and India toward "millions of robots by 2030" (company claims).

Wandercraft (France)

  • Exoskeleton maker (Atalante X rehab system; Eve self-balancing personal exoskeleton targeted for market from 2026) that pivoted its dynamic-walking expertise into industrial humanoids.
  • Calvin-40 industrial humanoid developed in 40 days with Renault Group; Renault holds a minority stake. Series D of $75M (equity+debt), 2025-06, incl. Renault, France 2030/Bpifrance PSIM fund.
  • Deployment roadmap: ~10 Calvins in Renault plants by end-2026, 350 robots across French and Spanish factories by end-2027 (plan, as of 2025-06). Calvin is headless by design — an early data point that industrial buyers don't pay for anthropomorphism.

PAL Robotics (Spain)

  • Founded 2004; Europe's longest-running humanoid vendor (REEM, TALOS research humanoid, ARI, TIAGo family).
  • Kangaroo bipedal lower-body/humanoid moved from prototype to purchasable product in 2025; shown at Automatica, IROS 2025 and Humanoids 2025 (Seoul).
  • New AI-ready robotic arm platform announced 2026-05, to be fully unveiled at ICRA 2026 (Vienna) with sales later in 2026. Primarily a research/EU-project-funded business rather than a venture-scale humanoid bet.

Humanoid (UK)

  • London startup founded 2024 by Artem Sokolov; ~$50M founder-led capital and ~175 staff (as of 2025-09, company figures).
  • HMND 01 Alpha wheeled dual-arm mobile manipulator unveiled 2025-09 (built in 7 months): 220 cm reach envelope, 15 kg two-arm payload, 7.2 km/h; RaaS business model. Beta planned Q3 2026; a bipedal variant is on the roadmap (unverified).

Europe — reading the region

  • Europe's strength is industrial demand-side pull (automotive OEMs like Renault, Bosch/Schaeffler as strategic investors) and EU/state co-investment (EIB, France 2030), not consumer robots — 1X is the exception and it relocated manufacturing to California.
  • No European company yet trains frontier-scale VLA models in-house at US/China scale; NEURA's Neuraverse is the most explicit attempt at a European data/skill ecosystem. See Investment for round-size comparisons.
  • Europe's largest closed physical-AI rounds of 2026 are not humanoid makers: Wayve (London, autonomous driving / embodied AI) closed a $1.2B Series D at $8.6B post-money on 2026-02-25 (plus $300M milestone-based Uber capital, $1.5B total secured), and AMI Labs (Paris, Yann LeCun's world-model lab) closed a $1.03B seed at $3.5B pre-money on 2026-03-09 — the largest European seed round on record. Contrast with NEURA's milestone-contingent "up to $1.4B". See Investment and World models.

Japan

National strategy & the foundation-model JV

  • "Japan AI Foundation Model Development" JV announced 2026-04-13: SoftBank Corp., NEC, Sony Group and Honda, with backing from Nippon Steel, Kobe Steel and three major banks; up to ¥1 trillion (~$6.1–6.3B) in NEDO funding over five years to build a domestically trained, trillion-parameter foundation model aimed at Physical AI (robotics, AVs, factory automation).
  • The operating company is now named Noetra; on 2026-06-30 NEDO formally selected the Noetra–AIST proposal under its "Multimodal Foundation Models for AI Robots and Physical AI" program, with ¥387.3B (~$2.4B) in FY2026 subsidies as the first tranche of the up-to-¥1T five-year total.
  • Roles: SoftBank + NEC train the models; Sony + Honda handle deployment into sensors, vehicles and robots. Honda is reported to contribute >2.3M hours of teleoperated driving data plus ASIMO-lineage manipulation traces; Sony contributes image-sensor datasets (press reports, unverified).
  • Japan's national robotics strategy (announced 2026-07-01) targets ~10 million AI-powered robots deployed across 18 industries by 2040, explicitly framed as an answer to labor shortage. See World models and VLA models for what such a model would need.

Toyota Research Institute (TRI)

  • TRI is Japan's strongest robot-learning lab, home of the Large Behavior Model (LBM) research program (diffusion-policy multitask manipulation; Russ Tedrake VP of Robotics Research) — see State of the art.
  • 2024-10: joint research partnership with Boston Dynamics (co-led by Tedrake and Scott Kuindersma). 2025-08-20: first public demo of an LBM driving Atlas — a ~450M-parameter Diffusion-Transformer / flow-matching policy producing whole-body manipulation + locomotion (walking, crouching, lifting) in long continuous sequences with no task-specific programming.
  • Toyota is also an early external trial user of Rainbow Robotics' RB-Y1 (as of 2026-06, single report, unverified).

Industrial incumbents

CompanyHumanoid/AI posture (as of 2026-06)
FANUCNo humanoid; showing LLM/plain-language cobot programming (Automate 2026); dominant in CNC + industrial arms
YaskawaAcquired Tokyo Robotics (100%, announced 2025-10) — Torobo wheeled dual-arm humanoid (Waseda spinout, torque sensing/impedance control); MOTOMAN NEXT adaptive cells on NVIDIA stack
Kawasaki Heavy IndustriesKaleido gen-9 humanoid shown at iREX 2025; first pilots targeted 2026
HondaASIMO line retired as a product; robotics know-how folded into the ASIMO OS brand (0-series EVs) and the 2026 foundation-model JV
  • The strategic picture: Japan retains the world's densest industrial-robot supply chain (FANUC, Yaskawa, Kawasaki, plus servo/reducer suppliers — see Hardware) but has no venture-scale humanoid champion; Chinese humanoid vendors began actively selling into Japan in 2025–26 (see the JAL Haneda trial below). Japan's bet is sovereign models + incumbents' manufacturing rather than startups.

JAL × GMO AI & Robotics: humanoid trial at Haneda

  • Japan Airlines (via ops subsidiary JAL Ground Service) + GMO AI & Robotics (GMO Internet Group's AI/robotics unit) announced 2026-04 what JAL calls Japan's first airport humanoid-robot demonstration: trials at Tokyo Haneda running 2026-05 through 2028, with robots deployed progressively across the airport.
  • Initial phase: two units on container transport and opening/closing container-securing levers. Target tasks: baggage/cargo loading, ground-support-equipment (GSE) operation, and cabin cleaning; human supervisors retain control of safety functions. Framing is explicit labor-shortage relief — record inbound tourism against a shrinking aviation workforce.
  • Hardware is Unitree G1-based (~1.32 m, ~35 kg, ~2 h per charge) — a Chinese platform working inside Japan's flagship airport, the concrete case of the Chinese-vendors-into-Japan dynamic above. Per-unit cost is reported inconsistently: ~$13.5K (base-variant figure per New Atlas) vs ~$15.4K (per CNBC). See Humanoid robots for G1 platform detail.

South Korea

National AI strategy: the ">$1T triple axis" (2026-06-29)

  • President Lee Jae Myung announced (2026-06-29), at the "3 Mega-Projects for Korea's Great Leap" national briefing (대한민국 대도약 3대 메가프로젝트 국민보고회, held at the presidential guest house 영빈관), a public-private investment drive totaling more than $1 trillion: "Semiconductors, physical AI, and AI data centers are the triple axis for our great leap forward." Won figures below are primary-verified against the Industry Ministry (산업통상부, MOTIE) press release of 2026-06-29 and korea.kr policy briefings; Industry Minister Kim Jung-kwan presented the "3S+1F" strategy.
  • Semiconductors: ₩800T — Samsung + SK (with suppliers) build four new fabs (two per company, ~₩400T each) plus supplier ecosystem in the southwest; exact fab sites were still undecided as of 2026-07 — Gwangju (Cheomdan-3 district or the military-airport site) is the leading candidate for Samsung's, South Jeolla sites (Jangseong/Haenam/Muan) for SK's (per Financial News Korea) — alongside accelerated Seoul-region fab completion (timelines shortened 7–12 years) — plus ₩81T for a Chungcheong packaging hub (new HBM fabs in Onyang/Cheonan, HBM packaging in Cheongju), and ₩30T+ over 15 years for next-gen semiconductor R&D. Stated goal: double memory (DRAM) production capacity within five years.
    • Dollar-figure reconciliation: the apparent $518B-vs-$585B conflict is a scope difference, not a factual one. Al Jazeera/AP's $518B = the ₩800T southwest-fab component alone; Ars Technica (FT syndication) $585B, CNN/Reuters $576B, and Korea Herald's $583B (the paper's own conversion — the government stated won figures only) all ≈ the combined ₩881T (800T fabs + 81T packaging). Residual spread is won-conversion noise (implied rates ~₩1,505–1,545/$).
  • AI data centers: ₩550T from SK Group, GS Group and Naver — stage 1 of 8.4 GW (SK 5 GW, GS 2.4 GW, Naver 1 GW), SK expanding to 15 GW by 2035, 18.4 GW total planned. (Al Jazeera's "₩1,000T by 2035" scaling figure does not appear in the MOTIE release — unverified against primary.)
  • Physical AI: the MOTIE release calls physical AI/humanoids an "irreplaceable national strategic industry" (대체 불가능한 국가전략산업) — framing language in the release, not (so far as documented) a formal statutory designation under Korea's national high-tech strategic industry law. Concrete goals — primary-verified in the MOTIE release text: 1,000+ AI robots deployed into worksites annually (start year not stated in the release; 2027 per ZDNet Korea's "from next year"), 10,000 AI-robotics specialists trained over five years, data factories for ten industries (10대 업종별 데이터팩토리) + a sovereign robot foundation model (RFM) within three years, and a "global top-1 in physical AI by 2030" target. Reported from the briefing but not in the release text: 10 industry-specialized humanoids commercialized by 2028 (Korea Herald, ZDNet Korea, Ars; industries per ZDNet: chemicals, shipbuilding, display, home appliances, logistics, medical, hospitality — automotive/steel/battery explicitly not yet finalized). The National Growth Fund (국민성장펀드, via the Financial Services Commission) adds ₩16T in 2026 across six physical-AI-adjacent sectors (AI, robots, future vehicles, defense, semiconductors, batteries) under the M.AX Frontier Project (announced 2026-07-01).
  • The "Hyundai $5.8B" reported by Ars = Hyundai Motor Group's ₩9T Saemangeum (Jeonbuk) robot-AI-hydrogen hub, a commitment formalized earlier in 2026 (investment agreement with the state and Jeonbuk province 2026-02-27; financing MOU with four policy-finance institutions — KDB, IBK, Korea Eximbank, KODIT — signed 2026-04-06 as the KDB policy-finance council's "No. 1 project") and folded into the physical-AI axis: AI data center ₩5.8T (~50k GPUs, build 2027→2029), robot manufacturing + parts cluster ₩0.4T (30,000 robots/yr plant, build 2028→2029), renewables ₩1.3T, electrolyser plant ₩1T, hydrogen-AI demo city ₩0.4T. Note: this Korean 30k/yr plant is distinct from the US RMAC (below) despite the matching capacity figure, and only ~4% of the $5.8B is robot manufacturing per se.
  • Samsung exercised its call option (announced 2024-12-31, completed 2025-02) to raise its stake in Rainbow Robotics from 14.7% to ~35% for KRW 267B (~$181.6M), becoming controlling shareholder and consolidating it as a subsidiary. A Future Robotics Office reporting to the CEO's office was created, led by Jun-ho Oh (KAIST HUBO creator; Rainbow co-founder).
  • Rainbow Robotics (founded 2011 out of KAIST's HUBO lab) sells the RB-Y1: dual 7-DoF arms on a fast wheeled base, ~140 cm tall — a wheeled-humanoid research/industrial platform.
  • 2026-03: Samsung announced plans to convert all global production facilities into "AI-driven factories" by 2030, deploying RB-Y1 on its own lines with agentic AI across warehousing-to-shipment.
  • 2026-06: RB-Y1 reported to have entered a proof-of-concept at a Coupang warehouse — Korea's first commercial humanoid warehouse trial; larger order contingent on results (ETNews report 2026-06-14, also carried by Korea Herald; none of the three companies has publicly confirmed the pilot).

LG Electronics

  • 2025-01: acquired majority control (51%) of Bear Robotics (Silicon Valley service-robot maker) via call option; Bear becomes LG's commercial service-robot arm alongside industrial subsidiary RoboStar.
  • CES 2026 (2026-01): unveiled CLOiD, a home humanoid for the "Zero Labor Home" — 7-DoF arms, five-fingered hands, built on LG's "Affectionate Intelligence" stack.
  • 2026-06/07: established a CEO-level Robotics Business Center, signaling a shift from R&D to commercialization across home, commercial and industrial robots plus components (actuators) and a robot-data factory.

Hyundai Motor Group / Boston Dynamics

  • 2026-06: Hyundai agreed to buy SoftBank's remaining 9.65% of Boston Dynamics for $325M (put option from the 2021 deal), taking 100% ownership.
  • CES 2026 strategy: Robot Metaplant Application Center (RMAC) opens 2026 in the US; robot manufacturing capacity of 30,000 units/year by 2028; Atlas deployed at Hyundai Metaplant America (Georgia) from 2028 (parts sequencing), expanding to assembly by 2030. Funded within Hyundai's $26B US investment (2025–2028) and KRW 125.2T Korea investment over five years from 2026.
  • Atlas's entire 2026 production run is committed to RMAC and Google DeepMind (Gemini Robotics on Atlas) — Boston Dynamics itself sits in Landscape: USA, but its owner, capital and factory roadmap are Korean.

Doosan Robotics & the K-Humanoid Alliance

  • Doosan Robotics (Korea's #1 cobot maker) declared a 2025 pivot from hardware-centric cobots to "practical humanoids": AI/SW and humanoid R&D orgs stood up in H2 2025, Innovation Center in Bundang opened 2025-09, US integrator ONExia acquired 2025-07, and a physical-AI collaboration with NVIDIA (announced 2025/2026, incl. AI-factory infrastructure across Doosan Group).
  • K-Humanoid Alliance (launched 2025-04-10 by MOTIE at the Plaza Hotel, Seoul; primary-verified): ~40 organizations at launch (robot makers, parts suppliers, AI labs, demand-side companies; later coverage counts 50+), with combined public-private investment of over KRW 1 trillion by 2030 across R&D, fund formation and M&A, toward making Korea a top humanoid nation by 2030; Doosan, Rainbow and academic labs participate. Korea's edge: actuators, batteries, semiconductors and chaebol factories as first customers.
  • Separately, MSIT (과기정통부) launched a "K-AI humanoid" public-private development consortium (2026-05-18): KIST-led, with LG Electronics, LG AI Research, LG Energy Solution, SNU, KAIST and Korea University — but at only ₩50.4B through 2030; do not conflate with the K-Humanoid Alliance or the mega-project figures above.

Rest of world

Israel

  • Mentee Robotics (Herzliya, founded 2022 by Amnon Shashua): MenteeBot full-size humanoid (~1.75 m, 25 kg payload, swappable battery >3 h in V3, unveiled 2025-02); serial production for logistics customers began mid-2025.
  • Mobileye agreed to acquire Mentee for $900M (announced 2026-01-06 at CES; $611.9M cash + 26,279,824 Mobileye Class A shares issued at closing; closed 2026-02-03), folding humanoids into Mobileye's "Physical AI" strategy while running Mentee as an independent unit. Series production/commercialization targeted for 2028. Shashua chairs both companies and recused himself from the board vote.

India

  • India ranks #3 globally in number of humanoid-robotics startups (~14, vs 28 US / 26 China, per Business Standard, as of 2025-12) — mostly local software + globally sourced hardware.
  • Early-stage robotics funding surged: >$45M in H1 2026, ~+120% YoY (aggregator estimate, unverified). Examples: iHub Robotics (Kerala; Tara Gen-1 semi-humanoid exported to UAE/Saudi; building a humanoid plant), Muks Robotics (Pune, bootstrapped humanoids).
  • India's bigger role may be as a manufacturing base: NEURA Robotics plans production scale-up across Germany and India (as of 2026-06).

UAE / Gulf

  • Abu Dhabi is positioning as a deployment sandbox: a full-size humanoid (from China's LimX Dynamics) is slated for 2026 pilots in Abu Dhabi malls/facilities after its BRIDGE Summit 2025 showcase.
  • Gulf sovereign capital increasingly appears on humanoid cap tables (e.g., via funds and strategic LPs); iHub's Tara robots already operate in UAE hospitality (single-source, unverified). See Investment.

Elsewhere

  • Canada: Sanctuary AI (Vancouver) continues dexterity-focused work (Phoenix humanoid, hydraulic hands) but has shifted away from a full-humanoid consumer narrative after 2024–25 leadership changes (unverified).
  • Norway–US, UK, Switzerland: strong robot-learning academia (ETH Zurich's ANYmal lineage → US spinouts; Oxford/Imperial → UK startups) feeds talent into the companies above; see Key people and Organizations.

Cross-regional comparison (as of 2026-06)

RegionFlagship betsCapital scaleDistinctive advantageMain gap
EuropeNEURA 4NE1, 1X NEO, Wandercraft CalvinNEURA ≤$1.4B; 1X ~$10B valuation (reported)Industrial OEM partnerships, state co-investmentNo frontier-scale in-house VLA training
JapanSovereign foundation-model JV; Kaleido, ToroboUp to ¥1T public over 5 yrsIndustrial-robot supply chain, data-rich incumbentsNo venture-scale humanoid startup
KoreaSamsung×Rainbow, Hyundai×Boston Dynamics, LG CLOiDNational ">$1T triple axis" (2026-06); Hyundai KRW 125.2T (group-wide); K-Alliance ≥KRW 1TChaebols as builder and first customer; componentsSoftware/AI models still largely externally sourced (e.g., DeepMind)
IsraelMobileye×Mentee$900M acquisitionAV-grade AI talent transfer to humanoidsSmall domestic market
IndiaiHub, Muks, contract manufacturing~$45M H1 2026 (early-stage)Cost, scale manufacturing potentialCapital depth, actuator supply chain
GulfDeployment pilots, sovereign LPsn/aRegulatory sandbox + capitalNo domestic builders yet

Open questions

  • Can NEURA convert an up-to-$1.4B round (tranched, condition-dependent) into actual 4NE1 volume shipments by late 2026? Tranche conditions are undisclosed.
  • Does Japan's consortium model (slow, state-funded, incumbent-led) produce a competitive robot foundation model before US/Chinese VLA models commoditize the layer?
  • Korea's humanoid AI is heavily partnered (DeepMind for Atlas); whether Samsung/LG build sovereign robot-AI stacks is a key 2026–27 watch item.
  • Whether 1X's home-first strategy survives contact with reliability/teleop-privacy economics — its 2025-12 industrial pivot deal suggests hedging. See Open problems.

Investment & Market

✓ 已核查更新 2026-07-04investment

Capital is flooding into Physical AI at a record pace: venture funding for robotics startups hit ~$15B in 2025 per Crunchbase — though broader-scope trackers count ~$26–28B (see the tracker note) — and had already reached ~$18.8B by mid-2026 (Crunchbase). Marquee deals define the cycle — Figure's $1B+ Series C at a $39B post-money valuation (2025-09), Skild AI's $1.4B at >$14B (2026-01), Physical Intelligence's $600M at $5.6B (2025-11) with a reported follow-on at $11B+ still unclosed as of 2026-07 — while China channels state capital (a ~¥1T NDRC robotics/AI fund, >$26B in city-level funds) and opens the IPO exit route (Unitree's STAR Market IPO registration approved 2026-07-02 at a ~$5.9B valuation). The gulf between valuations and actual revenue (Unitree, the segment's largest discloser, booked only ~¥1.69B ≈ $235M in 2025) fuels credible bubble warnings, including from China's own NDRC and from robotics pioneer Rodney Brooks.

Headline numbers (as of 2026-07)

  • Robotics venture funding: ~$15B in 2025, up from $8.2B in 2024 and above the prior 2021 peak ($14.1B per Crunchbase); $18.8B raised in 2026 through mid-year — on pace to more than double 2025 (Crunchbase). These are Crunchbase-scope figures; PitchBook counts $27.6B for 2025 under a broader "robotics & physical AI" definition — see Why 2025 totals differ by tracker.
  • Humanoid-specific venture funding: ~$3.2B globally in 2025 per Dealroom (part-year figure via Sifted, unverified) — more than the previous six years combined.
  • Most valuable private company: Figure AI at $39B post-money (as of 2025-09), among the ~20 most valuable startups in the world.
  • Largest single rounds to date: Saronic $1.75B Series D at $9.25B (2026-03, defense autonomy), Skild AI $1.4B (2026-01), Neura Robotics up to $1.4B (2026-06, milestone-contingent), Wayve $1.2B Series D (2026-02, autonomous driving), AMI Labs $1.03B seed (2026-03, world models), Figure $1B+ (2025-09), World Labs $1B (2026-02, world models). A widely repeated "Shihang Intelligent $1B Series A" (Crunchbase, 2026-06) is a currency error — the round was >¥1B RMB ≈ $140M (see China table).
  • First major pure-play humanoid IPO: Unitree Robotics, CSRC registration approved 2026-07-02, raising ¥4.2B (~$618M) on Shanghai's STAR Market at a ~¥40B (~$5.9B) valuation.

Why 2025 totals differ by tracker

2025 robotics-funding totals diverge by ~2x across trackers; the spread is methodology, not disagreement about the deals themselves.

Tracker2025 totalScope / notes
Crunchbase~$14B as first reported (2026-02), revised to ~$15B by mid-2026Narrower "robotics" industry tag; excludes most autonomous-vehicle and parts of drone/defense funding; totals drift upward as late-reported rounds are added retroactively
PitchBook$27.6B across 1,009 deals (vs $13.7B / 851 deals in 2024)"Robotics & physical AI" vertical — includes defense & security robotics ($8.0B across 234 deals in 2025), drones and autonomy
Dealroom~$26.5B (cited in secondary coverage; primary report paywalled, unverified)Broad robotics + physical-AI definition; under the same methodology Dealroom counts $55.8B for 2026 YTD — "nearly double" its full-2025 total
  • Even larger figures (~$40.7B for 2025) circulate when physical-AI-adjacent categories — AV platforms, world-model labs like World Labs and AMI Labs — are folded in (unverified).
  • Divergence drivers: scope (do AVs, drones, defense autonomy, world-model labs count as "robotics"?), timing (retroactive data additions), and occasional data-entry error (e.g., Shihang's ¥1B round recorded as $1B). Always cite the tracker alongside the number.

Neura Robotics (DE)Series Cup to $1.4B~$7B (reported)2026-06Tether, Nvidia, Amazon, Qualcomm, Bosch, Schaeffler, European Investment Bank (largest European robotics round; announced 2026-06-10). Full $1.4B is contingent on undisclosed performance milestones (CNBC + company release) — a ceiling, not cash banked
Wayve (UK)Series D (closed)$1.2B (+$300M milestone-based Uber capital = $1.5B total secured)$8.6B2026-02-25Eclipse, Balderton, SoftBank Vision Fund 2 co-leads; Microsoft, Nvidia, Uber; Mercedes-Benz, Nissan, Stellantis; OTPP, Baillie Gifford — autonomous driving / embodied AI
World Labs (US)Growth round (closed)$1B~$5.4B post (reported; company did not confirm — Bloomberg had reported ~$5B during talks)2026-02-18Autodesk ($200M lead); AMD, Nvidia, Emerson Collective, Fidelity, Sea. Fei-Fei Li's spatial-intelligence/world-model lab; total raised $1.23B
AMI Labs (FR)Seed (closed)$1.03B$3.5B pre-money2026-03-09Co-led by Cathay Innovation, Greycroft, Hiro Capital, HV Capital, Bezos Expeditions; Nvidia, Samsung, Temasek, Toyota Ventures. Yann LeCun's world-model lab; largest European seed round
Mind Robotics (US)Series A + follow-on$500M + $400Mn/d2026-03/05(unverified, single source — Crunchbase)

Context rows: Saronic (maritime autonomy/defense) raised a $1.75B Series D in 2026-03 — a reminder that "robotics funding" totals include defense autonomy, not just humanoids/manipulation. Wayve, World Labs and AMI Labs are included on the same logic: broad-scope trackers count AV platforms and world-model labs in "physical AI" totals, and their 2026 mega-rounds (~$3.2B combined, all closed) drove much of the H1-2026 record.

CompanyEventAmountValuationDateNotes

Pricing/subscription still pending as of 2026-07-04: SSE has 5 working days to clear the underwriting plan before the offering launches; debut expected late 2026-07 to early 2026-08
